resources.string: Quick launcher, command panel, and more
Trigger actions by shaking your phone
Allow separate shake actions on lock screen; disabled when screen is off
Set exclusive shake actions for specific apps
Lower values are easier to trigger
Disable shake gestures in blacklisted apps
Use these shake actions while the lock screen is shown
Intercept notifications and show floating alerts
Intercept system notifications and show custom floating alerts
Hide the card in landscape; danmaku may still show
Keep danmaku alerts visible in portrait
Keep danmaku alerts visible in landscape
Notification access is required to intercept notifications
Overlay permission is required to display message reminders
Permissions are ready — turn on message reminders and send a test notification
Choose reminder apps and set per-app filtering rules
Only show notifications from selected apps. When none are selected, all apps are allowed.
Do not show reminders while using these apps
Hide message reminders while one of these apps is in the foreground.
No Do Not Disturb apps selected
Do not show message reminders while system Do Not Disturb is on
Set notification filter rules for this app
No conditions yet; all notifications follow the selected mode
Choose card, side bubble, danmaku, and more
Enable \"Danmaku messages\" above to configure danmaku theme
Launch the app in a small window, then read the message
Swipe from the screen edge to open app index
Required to detect the foreground app and automatically disable Cebian triggers in selected apps
Edge gestures run only when this is on
Allow unrestricted background activity so the gesture service is not killed
Could not open battery optimization settings. Please whitelist this app manually in system settings.
Failed to save settings. Please try again later.
Works with battery optimization to improve background retention
Please enable auto-start permission manually in system settings
Automatically restore Cebian if the system disables it (requires WRITE_SECURE_SETTINGS)
Grant failed. Please run the ADB command manually.
Connect via USB or wireless debugging and run this command in a terminal (re-run after reinstalling the app):\n\n%1$s
When enabled, this app\'s settings screen won\'t appear in the system recents list
Required for edge gestures and sidebar panels; also used for Back, Home, Recents, and tap passthrough without Shizuku.
Log overlay FPS and main-thread slow messages in Logcat (Debug builds only)
Hide apps from the app index list
Hidden apps won\'t appear in Cebian panels, but can still be opened from the home screen and elsewhere.
Cebian edge triggers won\'t appear while you\'re using these apps.
Required for edge gestures and sidebar panels, plus Back, Home, Recents, and tap passthrough without Shizuku.
Each gesture slot can be configured independently, including app index, volume/brightness sliders, quick launcher, and more.
Adjust trigger angles for each direction
Adjust letter index height, columns, and panel opacity
Manage left and right edge triggers independently
Custom image support is coming soon
This removes the trigger and its gesture config on this side only.
Hold and slide to replay as Swipe from the pointer
Hold and slide to simulate touch in realtime from the pointer
Minimum Android version required is Android 16 (SDK 36)
Open the joystick action ring on long-press
Touch the screen to show a finger-following joystick (the whole circle is the control). Move the ring pointer; tap to click at the pointer. Bind the Floating pointer gesture to show it.
Higher values move the pointer faster. Sliding your finger across roughly this fraction of the screen width (or height) moves the pointer across the full screen horizontally (or vertically).

With preview on, an overlay appears on the real display. Use your configured edge gestures (e.g. swipe from the left edge) to preview joystick and travel bounds at each trigger position. Sliders update in real time. Dashed = full travel; solid = on-screen portion. The dot shows the pointer start when continuing from an edge.
Show joystick travel bounds on the real screen
Area between the inner ring edge and center dot
Color of the tap ripple effect
Pointer is only visible while touching the joystick
Maximum distance the joystick can move between press and release to count as a click or long-press. Increase in shaky environments.
Hide when tapping outside the joystick
Hide on a fast swipe gesture (e.g. page turn)
Hide after the joystick has been idle for a while
Configure joystick, pointer appearance, and movement
The rest of the screen stays interactive. Drag the joystick disc to move the pointer; tap to click; long-press to open the action ring (configurable in Joystick functions).
Preview and trigger actions when the pointer is pushed past screen edges
When the pointer nears a screen edge, a preview shows which action will fire if you keep pushing the pointer past the edge.
Show the action icon while approaching an edge
Add another configurable zone on this edge
Restore Quick Cursor default edge actions and preview settings
Long-press the joystick to show a ring of actions around it. Slide to a segment without lifting, then release to run that action.
When disabled, long-press dismisses the floating pointer.
Action executed when long-pressing the joystick
Simulate a swipe at the pointer position
Swipe from the current pointer position using the chosen direction and distance.
View live and archived notifications; hide and reopen
View live shade notifications and history
No notifications yet. Enable notification access to start recording.
This removes the record from the filter box. This cannot be undone.
This deletes every saved notification in the filter box. This cannot be undone.
Enable notification access for this app in system settings to record, hide, and reopen notifications.
The notification link memory has been recycled by Android and can no longer be opened.\nYou can open the app as an alternative.
Could not hide this notification from the shade
Restore notifications snoozed from the shade by this app.
Manage task rules from the Rules button in the filter top bar.
Hide rule removed; future matching notifications will show normally
Could not restore to shade; the notification may have been dismissed
Extract verification codes from notification text with keyword matching
Used when app rules do not match: find 4–8 digit codes near keyword matches.
Looks for 4–8 digit codes near keyword matches
Use the built-in default keyword pattern
Official rules loaded from smscode-rules: %1$d. Tap refresh for the latest.
No rules yet. Tap + to add.
Leave blank to apply to all apps
Name, keyword, and regex are required
Paste sample text to preview extraction
e.g. Your verification code is 123456, valid for 5 minutes.
Automatically fill the code into the focused input field
Fill the code after extraction succeeds
Try to tap confirm/submit after filling
Edge gestures and OTP auto-fill share the Cebian service
Fill order: LSPosed system inject → Cebian SET_TEXT
Cebian SET_TEXT is the fallback when inject fails or is disabled
Requires LSPosed module with android scope; simulates system keyboard input
Read SMS from the phone process; requires com.android.phone scope
Tap to check whether the inject hook is loaded in system_server
Cebian OTP: SMS hooks, system keyboard inject, Cebian auto-fill
No records for the current filter
Wait for a new SMS to arrive, or run a configured test flow.
Long numbers starting with 1068 are SMS sender IDs, not fill statistics. See Fill statistics at the bottom of the Extensions tab.
App rules are tried first; keyword fallback runs when none match. Use the test at the bottom to verify.
Needed to keep the background service running
Dragging trigger/index sliders shows a live layout preview; otherwise try gestures at the screen edge

Dragging handles or swipe-distance sliders shows a live preview: orange is the trigger zone, inner ring is short swipe, outer ring is long swipe; white arrows animate configured gestures; blue is the back-gesture intercept zone
Match this group鈥檚 handles to the same position on the opposite side
Reset handle design to the default style
Keep this group\'s design in sync with the opposite side
Uses a system API; may have compatibility issues
Current system API limit is 200 dp
Configure freeform launch mode and size
When on, freeform and related launch policies are available
This app is portrait-only; freeform preview and launch use portrait sizing
Live preview and customize freeform windows
OEMs launch freeform via ActivityOptions windowingMode with different values (Flyme 11, HyperOS 5, etc.). If it fails, enable freeform/free window in system settings.
For OriginOS and systems that support freeform sharing
Open fullscreen when you release the icon
Open in freeform when you release the icon
Fullscreen by default; hold the icon for the set time, then release to open freeform
Freeform by default; hold the icon for the set time, then release to open fullscreen
Hold an app icon this long, then release to use the alternate open method
Hide edge triggers in these apps
Configure short and long swipe actions
Show a volume bar and adjust while swiping
Show a brightness bar and adjust while swiping
Used when a gesture has no mode of its own
Run the action after you lift your finger
Run as soon as the swipe distance is reached
Edge wave stretches with the gesture
Bubbles pop inward from the edge
Choose the edge gesture hint animation
After selecting a style, tap the gear for color and shape options.
Prevents going off-screen; turn off to center
Change shape as your finger moves
After granting, you can dismiss recents cards, close apps, and use shell-based edge tap-through (recommended).
Same sidebar layout as the overlay panel: + to add, − to edit; in edit mode drag to reorder or tap the red badge to delete.
Tap Back to close 路 system bottom gestures still work
Tap to create a custom shortcut
Configure system widgets shown in the “Widget popup” gesture. Tap an empty cell to pick from the list; long-press an added widget to remove it.
Configure system widgets in the popup
Frosted glass behind the panel on Android 12+
Run shell commands via Shizuku and view output
Save common shell commands for one-tap run
Shell service outdated (api=%1$d). Update in Shizuku
Failed to restart shell service. Check Shizuku
Tap + at the bottom right to add a command. Tap a card to run; long-press to edit or delete.
Pipes and redirects work, e.g. dumpsys connectivity | grep wlan0
Timed out. Restart Shizuku and retry, or check whether the command blocks for a long time.
No commands yet — tap “Add command” below
Tap to close · long-press to copy output
Shizuku permission is required to show recent tasks

On Meizu / Flyme, match XposedSmsCode and enable the 4 LSPosed-recommended scopes: Phone services, Telephony storage, Flyme system services, and System framework. Do not enable this app itself. Restart the phone after changing scope.
Export or import app configuration for device migration
Backups include gestures, shake, and extension settings. You can optionally include OTP records and notification history (sensitive).
Include OTP records and notification history
Sensitive data stays in your local backup file only; nothing is uploaded.
Export or import a JSON configuration file
Export failed. Please try again later.
Import failed. Please check the file format.
Note: This backup contains sensitive data (%1$s). Import will overwrite current records.
Confirm import? This operation cannot be undone.
Cebian enhances your phone with edge gestures, shake actions, and notification tools. A few system permissions are required next.
Needed for edge triggers, side panels, and floating message reminders.
Core permissions are configured. Tune gestures on Home or import an existing backup from Extensions.
How data is handled and whether it is uploaded
Local data handling and permission purposes

Cebian is designed around on-device processing.
• Notifications and OTP: read, filtered, extracted, and displayed locally only. Nothing is uploaded by default. • OTP records and notification history: stored in app-private storage and excluded from system backup by default; optional export in settings backup. • LSPosed / Shizuku: optional enhancements that you enable yourself. • We do not collect personally identifiable information or send this data to developer servers.
Foreground service (special use): OverlayService keeps edge gestures and shake detection alive with subtype overlay_sidebar\u2014not for ads or unrelated background work.
Questions: open a GitHub Issue on the project repository.
OverlayService uses specialUse (overlay_sidebar) for edge gesture keep-alive and accessibility watchdog—not ads or unrelated background tasks.
View the latest features and fixes on GitHub
